---
title: "MoonDataR Package Documentation"
author: "Anuli Segree"
output: html_document
---

# MoonDataR Package

## Introduction

The MoonDataR package is a beginner-friendly R package designed to help students and new R users quickly explore and understand datasets.

The goal of this package is to simplify common data exploration tasks such as summarizing variables, checking for missing values, cleaning column names, detecting outliers, and creating basic visualizations.

## Why I Created This Package

During this course, we worked with R functions, data frames, packages, documentation, GitHub, and R Markdown. I wanted my final project to bring those skills together into one practical tool.

MoonDataR is meant for beginner R users who may not yet remember every command needed to inspect a dataset. Instead of writing many separate lines of code, a user can run a few simple functions to understand their data.

## Installation

```r
devtools::install_github("AnuliSegree/MoonDataR/MoonDataR")
library(MoonDataR)
```

## Example Dataset

The examples below use the built-in `iris` dataset.

```r
data <- iris
```

## Functions

### summarize_data()

```r
summarize_data(iris)
```

### missing_report()

```r
missing_report(airquality)
```

### clean_names_simple()

```r
clean_names_simple(iris)
```

### detect_outliers()

```r
detect_outliers(iris, "Sepal.Length")
```

### plot_numeric()

```r
plot_numeric(iris, "Sepal.Length")
```

### plot_categorical()

```r
plot_categorical(iris, "Species")
```

### make_data_profile()

```r
profile <- make_data_profile(iris)
print(profile)
```

## S3 Class

This package uses an S3 class called `moondata_profile`.

The function `make_data_profile()` creates the object and `print.moondata_profile()` controls how it is displayed.

## Libraries

This package uses `ggplot2` for visualization.

## DESCRIPTION Metadata

The DESCRIPTION file includes the package name, version, author, description, dependencies, license, and GitHub links.

## License

This package uses the CC0 license.

## Challenges

One challenge was learning how all parts of an R package work together, including DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE, functions, and GitHub.

## Future Improvements

Future versions may include more plots, automated reports, and additional data-cleaning tools.

## Use of AI

I used AI as a learning support tool to help organize ideas, debug code, and improve clarity. I reviewed and tested everything myself.

## GitHub Link

https://github.com/AnuliSegree/MoonDataR
